nodes.label: Cover Image
nodes.description: A composite cover image composed of 6 vertical panel aerial/satellite photographs arranged side by side. Each panel depicts a distinct river system or landscape viewed from high altitude. Panel 1 (leftmost): A meandering river with sinuous curves through what appears to be a forested or vegetated landscape â€” monochrome or desaturated tones suggesting winter or bare terrain. Panel 2: A dark green aerial view of a river delta or tributary system cutting through dense vegetation, possibly a tropical or subtropical forest. Panel 3: A golden-brown arid landscape with a narrow river or channel cutting through sandy/desert terrain â€” warm sandy tones dominate. Panel 4: A brown/ochre toned aerial view of a wide, shallow braided river or floodplain with multiple channels visible. Panel 5: A similar arid/semi-arid braided river system in warm brown tones, possibly a continuation or variation of panel 4. Panel 6 (rightmost): A white/grey monochrome aerial view of a river delta or dendritic drainage network against a pale background, possibly snow-covered or salt flat terrain. Overall theme: aerial perspectives of river geomorphology across diverse climate and biome types â€” tropical, arid, temperate, and polar/snowy environments. Likely used as a decorative or thematic cover for a document, project, or website related to geography, hydrology, remote sensing, or environmental science.
nodes.subject: river systems and landscapes

nodes.label: Visual Signposting
nodes.description: A reference image listing and describing examples of visual signposting techniques used in web and content design.

nodes.label: Navigation Menus
nodes.description: Well-organized menus are crucial for guiding users through a website. They should be easy to read and prioritize key sections.

nodes.label: Breadcrumbs
nodes.description: Breadcrumbs provide a visual trail showing users where they are on a website and how to navigate back.

nodes.label: Buttons and CTAs (Call-to-Actions)
nodes.description: Strong, clear call-to-action buttons help guide users to their next step. These are highly visual elements.

nodes.label: Headings and Subheadings
nodes.description: Clear headings break up content and make it easier to scan and find relevant information.

nodes.label: Footer Navigation
nodes.description: Footer navigation can provide a helpful backup for users at the end of a page.

nodes.label: Linked Images
nodes.description: Links on images can act as highly visual cues, encouraging users to explore further. For example, linking a product image directly to a product page.

nodes.label: Grid Galleries
nodes.description: Grid galleries display multiple sections of a site, offering displays of several images or pieces of content that link to different sections.

nodes.label: Visual Transitions
nodes.description: Visual transitions use images or animations to signal a transition to a new page or section, enhancing the user experience.

nodes.label: Infographics
nodes.description: Infographics can visually outline key points or steps in a process, making complex information easier to digest.

nodes.label: Visual Signposts in Videos
nodes.description: Using graphics or text in videos to highlight key points or signal what is coming next can enhance clarity in video content.

nodes.label: Landing Page Anatomy Diagram
nodes.description: Annotated wireframe and visual guide showing the recommended structure and sections of a high-converting landing page, with SEO and UX notes.
right_panel_bullets: Navbar: top of page with logo and navigation links
Hero: main section with header text, subheader text, and captivating imagery
Social proof: logos of press coverage or well-known customers
Call-to-action (CTA): signup button and concise incentive
Features and objections: key value propositions fully written out

nodes.description: A close-up photograph of a white or off-white wrinkled and crumpled paper or poster surface. The texture shows visible creases, folds, and subtle shadowing from the deformations. No text, figures, or distinct subject matter is present. Likely used as a background or texture asset.

nodes.norm_label: background texture image (ambiguous: wall or ceiling surface)

nodes.label: White Cracked Paint Texture on Wood
nodes.description: Close-up photographic texture of white cracked and peeling paint on a wooden surface. The weathered paint shows an extensive network of fine cracks and crazing patterns, revealing underlying wood grain. Monochromatic (black and white or heavily desaturated). Likely used as a background, overlay, or design texture element.
nodes.visual_content: AMBIGUOUS â€” purpose (decorative texture vs. documentary photo) not determinable from image alone
nodes.norm_label: white cracked paint texture on wood

nodes.label: Why-What-How Framework
nodes.description: A structured approach to creating content, comprising Hook, Body, and Outro+CTA sections

nodes.label: Hook
nodes.description: Opening section of the framework. Goals: confirm the video is not clickbait, set right expectations, establish singularity

nodes.label: Open Loop (For Body)
nodes.description: Transitional element within the Hook section. Example: 'But X is extremely difficult... and one of the biggest problems people fall into is Y...'. This is the first point of the video.

nodes.label: Open Loops
nodes.description: Retention mechanism. Why should the viewer continue watching? Creates intrigue to make the viewer watch until the Body starts.

nodes.label: Body
nodes.description: Main content section of the framework. Contains repeating Payoff units structured as Why, What, How.

nodes.label: Payoff Unit (Why / What / How)
nodes.description: Repeating structure inside the Body. Why: explain the reason behind the first key point. What: the solution or benefit offered. How: how will the viewer implement this information? Repeat for all Payoffs.

nodes.label: Payoff #1 Open Loop
nodes.description: An open loop placed within the Body at the first Payoff position. Creates curiosity by hinting at what will be revealed next.

nodes.label: Outro + CTA
nodes.description: Closing section. Goals: make a point connecting to the current video content, ensure the viewer understands the specific transformation they'll experience by clicking the next video.

nodes.label: Bad Signposting Example â€” Arrow Graphic
nodes.description: A landing page design critique image showing a mobile product page for 'Beam Dream' sleep supplement with arrow-based annotation signposting overlaid. Labeled as a 'bad' signposting example, using arrows and text callouts to indicate recommended UX/conversion elements.
nodes.norm_label: bad signposting example a€” arrow graphic

nodes.label: Beam Dream Product Page
nodes.description: Mobile e-commerce product page for 'Beam Dream', a sleep supplement. Shows product imagery, star rating (5-star, 3,144 reviews), product benefits, flavor/strength variants, quantity selector, frequency/subscription options, and Add to Cart CTA.

nodes.label: Add CTA to Bar (Annotation)
nodes.description: Arrow annotation pointing to the top announcement/promotion bar of the product page, recommending 'Add CTA to bar' as a conversion element.

nodes.label: Carousel Image Thumbnails (Annotation)
nodes.description: Arrow annotation on the left side pointing to carousel image thumbnails, labeled 'Carousel image thumbnails'.

nodes.label: Peek Through Next Image (Annotation)
nodes.description: Arrow annotation recommending 'Peek through next image' as a carousel UX pattern to indicate scrollability.

nodes.label: Reviews (Annotation)
nodes.description: Arrow annotation pointing to the star rating and review count area, labeled 'Reviews'.

nodes.label: Show Benefits/Feature of Product (Annotation)
nodes.description: Arrow annotation pointing to the product benefits checklist area, labeled 'Show benefits/feature of product'.

nodes.label: Style Swatches Easy to Switch Between (Annotation)
nodes.description: Arrow annotation on the right side pointing to flavor/style variant swatches, labeled 'Style swatches should be easy to switch between'.
nodes.norm_label: style swatches easy to switch between (annotation)

nodes.label: Variants Easy to Switch Between (Annotation)
nodes.description: Arrow annotation on the left pointing to strength variant buttons (Regular Strength / Extra Strength), labeled 'Variants should be easy to switch between'.

nodes.label: Add Bundles â€” Increases AOV (Annotation)
nodes.description: Arrow annotation on the right pointing to the quantity/pack selector (1 Pack, 2 Pack, 3 Pack), labeled 'Add bundles (increases AOV)'.

nodes.label: Add Subscriptions â€” Increases LTV (Annotation)
nodes.description: Arrow annotation on the right pointing to the frequency/subscription selector (Subscribe & Save 20% Off), labeled 'Add Subscriptions (increases LTV)'.

nodes.label: Add Daily Cost to Consume Product Under CTA (Annotation)
nodes.description: Arrow annotation pointing below the Add to Cart button, recommending 'Add daily cost to consume your product & shipping time under the CTA'.
nodes.norm_label: add daily cost to consume product under cta (annotation)

nodes.label: Add Answers to Visitor Questions (Annotation)
nodes.description: Arrow annotation on the bottom right pointing to FAQ accordion sections (Benefits, Ingredients, How To Use, Shipping & Returns), labeled 'Add answers to answer visitors question'.

nodes.label: websiteslandingpages Brand Watermark
nodes.description: Brand/watermark logo in the top-left corner reading 'websiteslandingpages', indicating the creator or publisher of this annotated signposting example graphic.

nodes.label: Arrow-Based Annotation Method (Bad Signposting Pattern)
nodes.description: The overall annotation method used in this graphic: freeform text labels connected to UI elements via arrows on a dark blue background. This is designated a 'bad' example of signposting in academic or instructional writing contexts, likely because the arrows create visual clutter, lack clear hierarchy, or are not a standard academic signposting convention. AMBIGUOUS: The exact reason this is labeled 'bad' is not stated in the image itself.
